title: IntStack with operator+= Push and operator<< Print
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete `IntStack` so it supports pushing elements via `operator+=` and printing via `operator<<`.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 8) — number of elements to push
- Line 2: N integers separated by spaces

## Output
- Print the stack contents as `[a, b, c]` (bottom to top, comma-space separated), followed by a newline.
- An empty stack prints `[]`.

## Requirements
1. `IntStack` uses a fixed internal array `int m_data[8]` and an `int m_count` (initially 0).
2. `IntStack& operator+=(int value)` pushes `value` and returns `*this`. If `m_count == 8`, do nothing.
3. `friend std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& out, const IntStack& s)` prints `[a, b, c]` iterating from index 0 to `m_count - 1`; empty stack prints `[]`.
4. Hidden tests push exactly 1 element and exactly 8 elements (full capacity).

## Notes
- Only `<iostream>` is needed.

title: IntStack with bool, Pop, and Copy — Complete the Function
prompt_md:
## Goal
Extend `IntStack` by completing `explicit operator bool()`, prefix `operator--` (pop), and the copy constructor. The exercise combines multiple chapter 21 operators in one class.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 8) — number of elements to push
- Line 2: N integers
- Line 3: integer M (0 ≤ M ≤ N) — number of elements to pop from the copy

## Output
- Line 1: `original: [...]` — the original stack after all pushes (unchanged)
- Line 2: `copy_after_pops: [...]` — the copy after M pops
- Line 3: `copy_empty: 1` if the copy is now empty, `0` otherwise

## Requirements
1. Use the `IntStack` from exercise 1 (operator+=, operator<<) — they are already provided in the starter.
2. Complete `explicit operator bool() const` — returns `true` if `m_count > 0`.
3. Complete prefix `IntStack& operator--()` — decrements `m_count` if `m_count > 0`; returns `*this`.
4. Complete the copy constructor `IntStack(const IntStack& src)` — copies both `m_count` and `m_data`.
5. `main` pushes N values into the original, deep-copies to a second stack, pops M times from the copy, then prints as specified.
6. Popping from an empty stack must not crash or underflow (guard with `m_count > 0`).

## Notes
- Only `<iostream>` is needed.
